acceptance_results.criterion: docker-compose.yml points the acquisition-api build at a context that includes both services/acquisition-api and common.
services/acquisition-api/Dockerfile copies only required build inputs and builds with readonly module resolution.
evidence: DOCKER_CONFIG=<tmp> REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE=<tmp> docker compose --env-file .env.example -f docker-compose.yml -f /tmp/timwe-compose-auth-probe-override.yml build acquisition-api

acceptance_results.criterion: No Go source, dependency metadata, vendor, frontend, package manifest, or lockfile files are changed.

acceptance_results.blocker: Acquisition API exits during admin schema bootstrap because add_admin_management_tables.sql expects relation products.
acceptance_results.criterion: Full compose runtime health passes for acquisition-api.
acceptance_results.reason: This is a downstream runtime schema-order defect discovered after the image build fix, outside TMP-030 acceptance.

blockers: Acquisition API runtime probe exits during admin schema bootstrap because add_admin_management_tables.sql expects relation products.
Notification worker exits in compose on DB SSL mode mismatch.
Postback dispatcher retries against localhost DB instead of the compose database service.
cleanup_notes: Temporary compose override files were removed.
Temporary auth files were created under /tmp and removed by shell traps.
Temporary compose projects were stopped with docker compose down -v --remove-orphans.
completed_work: Changed acquisition-api compose build to use the repo root as context with an explicit services/acquisition-api/Dockerfile path.
Updated the acquisition API Dockerfile to copy common and service module inputs into paths matching the existing ../../common replacement.
Built the acquisition API compose image successfully using temporary isolated Docker auth.
Re-ran bounded compose smoke far enough to record downstream runtime blockers separately from image build readiness.
Updated the full-system release matrix and TMP-021 value gate with current compose evidence.
known_gaps: Acquisition API runtime health remains blocked by admin schema bootstrap ordering/schema dependency.
Notification worker and postback dispatcher compose runtime configuration blockers remain.
Real provider/env values are still required for live-flow proof after local runtime blockers are fixed.
operator_message: TMP-030 fixed the acquisition API compose image build. Compose now reaches app startup and exposes downstream runtime blockers, led by acquisition API admin schema bootstrap expecting products.
risk_notes: No dependency, vendor, Go source, frontend, package manifest, lockfile, credential, or schema files were changed.
Root build context increases sent Docker context size; a future .dockerignore optimization would be separate scope.
summary: Acquisition API compose build-context defect fixed and verified.

unfinished_work.blocker: Admin schema migration expects relation products before it exists in compose runtime.
unfinished_work.reason: Targeted runtime probe reproduced this after image build passed.

validation.command: DOCKER_CONFIG=<tmp> REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE=<tmp> docker compose --env-file .env.example -f docker-compose.yml -f /tmp/timwe-compose-auth-probe-override.yml build acquisition-api
bounded docker compose smoke with temporary auth, Redis override, and temporary shared-network
